title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Pull Platform Resolution
summary: A release team must prove exactly which immutable multi-layer artifact ran even after a mutable tag moves in the registry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker pull platform resolution increment in image identity and layer provenance ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Pull Platform Resolution · Theory Docker Pull Platform Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Pull Platform Resolution contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Pull Platform Resolution · Theory Docker Pull Platform Resolution.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull. For Predict Docker Pull Platform Resolution · Theory Docker Pull Platform Resolution, keep Docker Pull Platform Resolution connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Pull Platform Resolution while producing image identity and layer provenance ledger.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull. A defensible solution applies Docker Pull Platform Resolution, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to image identity and layer provenance ledger.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-pull-platform-resolution — Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-2
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: worked docker images layers registries 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Pulling one tag always downloads one architecture-specific image and every layer again. This fails the Docker Pull Platform Resolution contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.

steps.title: Build Docker Pull Platform Resolution evidence · Theory Docker Pull Platform Resolution
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-pull-platform-resolution-11o1te". Tailor its image-identity.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-pull-platform-resolution-11o1te
  - competency: docker-pull-platform-resolution
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: pull platform resolution
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: rootless remote builder on an arm64 staging host
    fault-injection: cancel during export after one platform finishes
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 11o1te
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
